data.name: Sourabh
data.niche: UPSC preparation, geopolitics, international affairs, political science
data.positioning: UPSC aspirant learning in public — breaking down complex geopolitical matters in simple words and keeping the audience updated on international news and current affairs.
data.audience_description: Mid to late 20s Indian UPSC aspirants who feel overwhelmed by current affairs and need guidance
data.audience_fear: Not clearing UPSC in multiple attempts
data.audience_aspiration: Guidance and a one-stop source for current affairs to crack UPSC
data.current_project: Actively preparing for UPSC. Reads extensively — books, blogs, Saarthi IAS for current affairs. Building a curious audience interested in current affairs and international relations, mostly UPSC aspirants.
data.origin_story: Decided to pursue UPSC since high school. Motivated by desire to serve the nation and have a good lifestyle as a civil servant. No major doubts — committed to the path. Currently reading 7-8 hours daily in active preparation.
hot_takes: Most UPSC courses are sold in recorded form and are outdated — they don't keep up with current affairs
Real UPSC preparation is about curiosity and learning, not just rote study
You need to see the world through a 'third eye' — understanding both right and left, all perspectives without bias
data.voice_style: Analytical and research-based tone. Uses quotes from notable sources. Balanced — avoids biased opinions. Aims to present multiple perspectives objectively.

style_rules.bias: strictly avoid one-sided or biased opinions
other_rules: cite or reference sources when relevant

private_topics: Insecurity about not being consistent at studies — never mention publicly
